items.q: What are conventions of the Gothic genre?
items.correct: Settings of ruin and decay, supernatural or psychological terror, transgression of boundaries, the sublime, doubled or haunted protagonists, and atmospheric excess.
items.misconception: Gothic just means scary stories with vampires and ghosts.
items.random_wrong: Gothic refers to medieval architecture, not literature.
items.why: Students reduce Gothic to monsters. The genre is defined by its concerns — boundaries, the past intruding on the present, the limits of reason — and only sometimes uses supernatural creatures.

items.correct: A fatal error or flaw in the tragic hero that leads to their downfall — sometimes a character defect, sometimes a mistake in judgement.
items.misconception: Hamartia is the tragic hero's evil personality.
items.random_wrong: Hamartia is the moment the tragic hero dies.
items.why: Tragic heroes are not villains. Hamartia is often a noble quality taken too far (Othello's love, Macbeth's ambition) or a misjudgement — not simple wickedness.

items.q: What is the structural framework of The Canterbury Tales?
items.correct: A frame narrative — pilgrims travelling to Canterbury tell stories along the way, allowing Chaucer to juxtapose different voices, genres and social classes.
items.misconception: It is one continuous story told by Chaucer from beginning to end.
items.random_wrong: It is a religious text used in Catholic services.
items.why: Students miss the frame structure. Chaucer's strategy is polyphony — voicing many speakers to compare and contrast — not a single narrative voice.

items.q: Which 1984 essay by Christopher Craft reads the vampire's mouth as a site of queered desire?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: 'Kiss Me with Those Red Lips'. “'The Occidental Tourist'” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Who stakes Lucy in the crypt at Kingstead?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Arthur Holmwood. “Jonathan Harker” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: How many female vampires (the 'sisters') attempt to seduce Harker at Castle Dracula?
items.why: Exact answer: 3. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
